INITIAL MESSAGE . . .

Slime people are classified as a myth. There are certain types of slime that have the ability to mimic other creatures, sure, but very rarely do they mimic humans. Not only that, but they’re easy to spot, due to their lack of intelligence and unnatural colors. It’s not everyday you see a bright green, shiny, jiggly person with dripping skin, after all.

Laios is intrigued by these creatures. Despite the ambiguity surrounding their existence, he’s curious to figure out if they’re real or not. Is their anatomy similar to a regular slime? Or is it more human? Do they have organs at all? Would they taste like regular slime? Or have their own distinct taste?

And, of course, there’s his more perverse thoughts, the ones he’s a bit more shameful of than his others — though, that’s not saying much, considering he’s not shameful at all.

In all his years of dungeon crawling, seeing party members come and go, witnessing hundreds of types of monsters, from slime to walking mushrooms to red dragons, he’s never seen a humanoid slime monster. It’s one of the highest things on his bucket list. To find one, feel it, talk to it, taste it, maybe even…

Laios stretches out his arms, yawning as he wakes up. It’s still the middle of the night, it seems, based off the fact everyone else is asleep and the dungeon sounds more quiet than ever. Though, there is a distant sound he can’t ignore. Running water.

That reminds him how dehytrated he is. It’s been a while since Laios has had some cool, clean water. There’s not many bodies of water in the dungeon, so everytime he finds one, he makes sure to fill up all of their waterskins before they move on.

Might aswell now that he’s awake.

Laios stumbles through the darkness of the dungeon, completely armorless as he figures there’s no need for it if he’s making a quick trip. The waterskins are in his arms, empty. He rounds a corner into a small opening, being greeted by a large pond of pretty, slightly glowing dungeon water…
